text:
Summary:
- DoD-facing contact center operator supporting critical comms (emergency dispatch, infra ops).
- Must deploy in completely air-gapped on-prem enclave maintained by customer SOC. No egress to public internet allowed; outbound updates require packaged media and manual approval.
- Primary features wanted: agent assist (real-time suggestions + canned responses), ticket summarization (post-call TL;DR), tool-calling integration (RA/TA to call internal CRM/dispatch tools), offline reranking for search across document stores, and automated playbook triggers for high-severity incidents.

Key constraints/requirements:
- Security: FIPS 140-2 HSM required for key management, SOC2+ISO27001 posture, detailed audit logs retained 7+ years, SIEM integration (Splunk), SSO via SAML and RBAC.
- Compliance: anticipating IL4/DoD STIG checks; vendor to provide SBOM, signed supply chain attestation...

text:
Status: mid-market product org evaluating Redwood. Short background: Bridgepoint builds a personalization and support layer that uses embeddings + reranking to power the in-product help center and recommended content. Started on Hosted API to validate latency/cost; moving to Dedicated for predictable throughput and data isolation.

POC summary: 14-day hosted POC completed. Key metrics: avg token cost (hosted) higher than target; latency within SLO for short chats (median 60ms) but occasional tail spikes at 95th pctl ~110-130ms. SE tuning (Priya) improved batching and cache hit rate to 42%. They want Dedicated for lower unit cost and better isolation.

Pricing/packaging discussion notes (primary focus):
- Bridgepoint wants 3-year preferred pricing but finance hesitant; leaning toward 12-24 month commit.
- Primary ask: commit-level to cover baseline 50qps equivalent (est. sustained thro...

Account background: mid-tier EU payments platform expanding into regulated lending. Primary concern = data residency and custodial separation. POC scope: private VPC deployment in eu-west-1, BYOK KMS integration, audit log shipping to customer SIEM, region locks for production.

text: Account summary: Helios Forge is a 3-year Redwood hosted customer using embeddings and chat for internal MLOps tools and plant support chatbots. They want to transition critical inference into a Dedicated/reserved model to meet 24/7 SLAs and reduce noisy tail-cost. Key asks: 1) Dedicated sizing for three reserved pools (low-latency and high-throughput profiles), 2) Optimize pilot for nightly rerank and batch embedding jobs (target >=25% cost reduction), 3) Private deployment option with control plane in Redwood but runtime on-customer-premises for Japanese factories to satisfy residency. Technical: current models are quantized LLaMA variants and a custom reranker; want compatibility with KV cache and efficient batching. Business: procurement prefers a 12-month commitment with staged ramp; requests 10-18% committed discount and clear SLA for burst scaling. Risks: procurement QBR in lat...

text:
Account summary:
- Polaris runs a B2B support outsourcing platform used by EU retail and fintech customers. High SLAs for agent UX (fast suggestions) and strict data residency for EU tenants.
- Primary objective: deploy RAG-backed agent-assist + self-serve deflection widget that returns precise top-5 docs for agents and an auto-suggest short answer for end-users.

Scale + infra expectations:
- Corpus estimate: 8M historical tickets + 16M chat snippets -> ~24M passages after chunking. Expected vector count: ~24M (1536-d). Estimated index footprint: ~2.5-3.5 TB depending on index format + quantization.
- Query profile: baseline 120 QPS, peak bursts to 500 QPS for campaign days; traffic is highly diurnal and tenant-segmented.
- Latency SLO: 95th percentile end-to-end search + rerank <= 180ms (strict internal requirement). Must have documented fallback behavior at >200ms.
- Relevance targ...

text: Account is a long-term hosted API customer (3 years). Exploring Dedicated to stabilize peak throughput for last-mile routing and telemetry summarization workloads. POC completed (two-node Dedicated) in Jan-Feb 2026; saw ~18–22% reduction in average latency for chat flows and 12% token-cost improvement when enabling prefix caching. Customer very interested in Redwood Optimize recommendations — SE reviewed batching + quantization playbook and customer asked for a 2-week pilot. EU data residency is gating renewal: ops/legal said 'no EU processing without residency guarantee'. Procurement timeline: target May sign. AE notes: need final SOW + amendment covering v2 residency clause and updated SLA for P99. Short-term ask: price alignment for burst capacity and explicit fallback routing to hosted during EU maintenance windows. PSR open for priority networking help (INF-1189).

title: Pelmont Converse Solutions
preview: Mid-market product analytics company building a user-facing chat assistant and semantic product search. Primary goals: predictable per-month inference cost, p95 latency <150ms for NA and <200ms for EU, throughput ~800-1,200 requests/sec for peak flows. Quality must match current LLM baseline for summarization and re...
text: Mid-market product analytics company building a user-facing chat assistant and semantic product search. Primary goals: predictable per-month inference cost, p95 latency <150ms for NA and <200ms for EU, throughput ~800-1,200 requests/sec for peak flows. Quality must match current LLM baseline for summarization and reranking tasks. Required features: token-level cost breakdown, per-route dashboards, rollouts/canary for model versions, SSO (SAML), audit logs and KMS integration. Preferred model families: smaller cost-efficient variants of open models (quantized 7B/13B) with pinning + automatic fallback to hosted variants during maintenance. Must support VPC/private networking for Dedicated deployment and contractual data residency in EU for certain customers.

CRM notes:
- Company: Ironbridge Medical AI (large regional health system running AI for clinician workflow).
- Primary pain: strict regulatory + residency requirements across US & EU, must be able to run inference in customer VPC.
- Key ask: Private VPC deployment with SSO (SAML/Okta), SCIM provisioning for automated user/groups, and fine-grained RBAC exposed in Redwood Console/control plane mapped to AD groups.
- Security stack: customer uses AWS (GovCloud for some workloads) + Splunk for SIEM. They require: customer-managed KMS keys (CMK), audit log streaming to Splunk/CloudWatch, 7-year retention for logs, and SOC2/ISO27001 evidence.
- Quote from CISO: "No console admin user that isn't bound to our AD groups — full RBAC and SCIM mapping is non-negotiable."
- Technical notes from SE call: need SAML metadata template, SCIM provisioning test plan, sample mapping for role -> AD group ...

STATUS: moving to procurement. Mid-market SaaS (B2B) building an AI-driven in-app support assistant + enhanced product search. Tried Hosted API first; POC OK but unit cost at projected scale pushes them toward Dedicated.

Key quotes
- "We want predictable monthly spend — spikes ok but need clear overage policy." — Head of Platform
- "Latency target: 150ms p95 for chat front-end, embeddings retrieval < 50ms for reranking."

POC findings (hosted):
- Re-ranking using 4k-context embeddings + knn performed well functionally; token costs will blow past $X at 1M queries/mo.
- Latency variability during peak hours ~200-400ms; not acceptable for live chat SLA.

Why Dedicated now:
- Need consistent p95 latency; desire for reserved GPU capacity and VPC connectivity for HIPAA-adjacent customers in their pipeline.
- Want clear ramp schedule: start with 2 reserved nodes (committed) month 1–3, add 4...

text:
Summary: CounselMesh is a mid-size e-discovery and compliance provider moving from a homegrown Elastic+vector plugin to a vendor-managed RAG stack. They are heavy on retention/audit requirements and want a private/VPC deployment with customer-managed keys.

Conversation highlights:
- \"We need verifiable audit trails for every query and admin action; retention policies must be enforceable per-case.\" (Head of Security)
- High indexing scale: currently ~120M documents (mixed PDFs, native docs, audio transcripts). Estimated growth to 250M over 18 months.
- Expected query profile: 30-50k embedding lookups/day, peak concurrent rerank pipelines ~600 qps. Overall latency SLO: <= 200ms for 1-to-5 candidate rerank path for top-5 results.
- Cost sensitivity: prefer cost-per-query model for embeddings+rerank, want tooling to project unit costs for different batching/quantization profiles.

text:
Account snapshot: Solaris Model Ops is an ecommerce personalization platform looking to move model serving for ranking + session-level chat to Redwood Dedicated. Heavy seasonality (Q4 peaks) — needs strict throughput guarantees and well-defined burst policies.

Short summary of needs and constraints:
- Primary workload: real-time reranking + candidate scoring (reranking) with occasional contextual chat for shopper assistance.
- Target p95 token latency: <= 120ms for text-generation endpoints (single-turn recommendation explanations).
- Minimum sustained throughput (negotiation): 25,000 tokens/sec across the dedicated cluster during normal peak; allow bursts up to 60,000 tokens/sec for up to 15 minutes during flash sales.
- Concurrency caps requested: 200 concurrent active sessions for ranking endpoints; 50 concurrent for long-chat sessions. Prefer per-route concurrency limits.
- Reser...

text:
AE notes: Good fit — product team wants predictable costs, clear upgrade path from Hosted -> Dedicated.

PM quote: 'We need a plan that lets us scale quickly in Q3 without surprising bills.'

SE notes: POC showed consistent latencies; need to validate KV cache reuse across sessions for chat context costs.

Pricing notes: Redwood proposal A = 12mo commit, $18k/mo committed, burst up to 3x for 72h windows, overage $0.0009/token; Proposal B = 6mo trial at $10k/mo with higher overage $0.0015/token.

Customer counter: prefers 9mo minimum, wants ramp schedule with credits first 3 months (50% usage credit month1, 25% month2), and hard overage cap at 15% of committed spend per month.

Finance ask: monthly invoicing, ability to convert to quarterly prepay at discounted rate; need clear cost model per 1k tokens for budgeting.

Security: require SSO/SAML, AWS KMS integration, audit logs retained...

Routing: policy-based routing by latency and cost tier; automatic fallback to cheaper model variant preferred.

Quick summary / CRM-style notes:
- Company: Silverdune builds an in-app product insights assistant for B2B SaaS customers (trial users -> paid). Mid-market customer base; fairly price sensitive but values reliability.

- Primary ask now: sizing & pricing for Dedicated reserved capacity. They want predictable per-request latency and to avoid noisy-neighbor surprises during feature launches.

- Strong requirement for VPC/private control plane + KMS integration. On-prem option for sensitive clients in EMEA.
- Cost sensitivity: wants clear unit economics per token / per-embedding and predictable monthly rate.

text:
Background: Arkhaven runs a clinical triage + patient Q&A layer across several hospital networks. Currently on Redwood Hosted API with steady growth (1.2B tokens/mo). Primary drivers for expansion: predictable latency for emergent triage workflows, data residency for EMR-integrated sites, and unit-cost reduction for large-batch summarization pipelines.

Commercial ask: 1) Pilot Dedicated (3x A100-equivalent pool) for latency-critical triage and trial matching; 2) Adopt Redwood Optimize for batching, prefix/KV caching, and quantization recommendations to reduce token spend and GPU-hours; 3) Add optional Private (on-prem package) for two hospital partners in Midwest with air-gapped requirements.

Security: Requested SSO/SAML, KMS integration (customer-managed key), SOC2 + ISO27001 evidence. Legal requested a KMS amendment and data residency clause for on-prem sites. Security already con...

They run AML/risk-monitoring workloads (embeddings + reranking) where data cannot leave EU. Key items discussed: - SIG/CAIQ: Cedarbridge sent partial CAIQ. They expect completed ...
text: CRM notes - Cedarbridge (FS, EU HQ). Primary ask: Private VPC deployment with customer-managed KMS and strict data residency in eu-west-1. They run AML/risk-monitoring workloads (embeddings + reranking) where data cannot leave EU. Key items discussed: - SIG/CAIQ: Cedarbridge sent partial CAIQ. They expect completed CAIQ back plus clarifications on a handful of control responses (data deletion, third-party subprocessors). - Shared responsibility: they require explicit matrix stating Redwood handles runtime, OS patching, model weights, but customer controls keys, network, and ingress policies. We drafted matrix and will formalize during security deep-dive. - KMS integration: customer-managed CMK in AWS KMS preferred. Need to validate key policies and cross-account grants; SE to provide example IAM policies. - Audit logging: retention 1 year minimum, logs exported to customer S3 account ...

text:
Account snapshot: global market-data provider; latency-sensitive trading assist and real-time enrichment. Key asks centered on ironclad SLA (uptime + credits), deterministic latency, reserved GPU planning and predictable maintenance windows.

SLA negotiation highlights:
- Customer requests: 99.99% uptime for primary trading endpoints, 99.95% for non-critical APIs. Wants credits to scale and not be de minimis.
- Redwood baseline: proposed 99.95% platform SLA for Dedicated; offered add-on for 99.99% with increased reserved capacity and dedicated engineer.
- Credit schedule discussed (customer pushed for formula). Current proposed sliding scale (example):
 * uptime >= 99.95%: 0% credits
 * 99.9% <= uptime < 99.95%: 10% of monthly Dedicated fee
 * 99.0% <= uptime < 99.9%: 30% credit
 * uptime < 99.0%: 100% credit (customer insisted on cap at 100% monthly fee)
- Exclusions: scheduled maint...

title: Cerium Vertex Infrastructure
preview: Private VPC-only deployment with no public egress for sensitive workloads,BYOK option + HSM support (customer-managed CMK) with key rotation policy,Audit logs: immutable, exportable, retention 7 years, access controls and SIEM integration (Splunk/SumoLogic),SIG/CAIQ completion required; gap remediation plan for item...
text: Private VPC-only deployment with no public egress for sensitive workloads,BYOK option + HSM support (customer-managed CMK) with key rotation policy,Audit logs: immutable, exportable, retention 7 years, access controls and SIEM integration (Splunk/SumoLogic),SIG/CAIQ completion required; gap remediation plan for items failing vendor intake,Shared responsibility matrix showing which telemetry and backups Redwood retains vs customer-owned storage,Regional data residency: keep training data and embeddings in region (EU for EU accounts) — ability to tag and restrict models per region,Network: support for VPC peering / PrivateLink / customer-managed NAT gateways; explicit IP allowlist for admin consoles,Operational SLOs: p95 latency targets per workload, canary rollouts for model version changes, incident escalation contact list

- POC summary: hosted API POC deemed functionally successful. Observed p95 tail when concurrent burst simulated; SE suspects cache warming + KV eviction under load.
- Model preference: open-family models OK (Redwood-verified profiles preferred). Must be able to pin model version and fallback to smaller variant under capacity pressure.

text:
Summary: Large EU-headquartered manufacturing automation vendor evaluating Redwood Private (VPC) for on-prem-like isolation. Primary drivers: data residency in EU, HSM-backed key control, demonstrable audit trails, and strict network isolation. They ran a 3-week POC (chat + document search) on Dedicated; now moving to procurement for private VPC.

Security intake / vendor risk: Legal & InfoSec requested full SIG/CAIQ deliverable. They want:
- Completed SIG questionnaire with evidence links (SOC2 cert, encryption diagrams).
- CAIQ mappings to our control implementations and a shared-responsibility table that their security team can drop into their vendor-risk system.
- Explicit network architecture: VPC peering vs. PrivateLink, NAT/egress controls, firewall rules, allowed IP ranges, and flow logs retention policy.

KMS / key management:
- Customer requires customer-managed keys (CMK) w...
